title: JÜRGEN SCHMIDHUBER - Professor, Computer Science
excerpts:
Section Title: JÜRGEN SCHMIDHUBER > ```
Content:
Prior to joining KAUST, he worked as Scientific Director of the Swiss AI Lab, IDSIA, and as professor of Artificial Intelligence (Ordinarius) at the University of Lugano (USI) from 2009-2021. He is most noted for his pioneering work in the field of artificial intelligence (AI), deep learning, and artificial neural networks. In 1990-91, he laid foundations of GenAI by introducing the principles of Generative Adversarial Networks (now used for deepfakes), unnormalised linear Transformers (see the T in ChatGPT), and self-supervised Pre-Training for deep learning (see the P in ChatGPT). His lab also produced LSTM, the most cited AI of the 20th century, and the Highway Net (a variant of which is the most cited AI of the 21st century). Elon Musk tweeted: "Schmidhuber invented everything." His AI is on over 3 billion smartphones, and used many billions of times per day.

title: AI Speaker Jürgen Schmidhuber | Aurum Speakers Bureau
excerpts:
Section Title: Jürgen Schmidhuber > Jürgen Schmidhuber biography
Content:
Jürgens’ team was the first to win official computer vision competitions using deep neural networks in 2011. They had the first deep neural network to win a medical imaging competition on cancer detection in 2012. Furthermore, his group developed mathematically rigorous universal artificial intelligence and metalearning. They also developed recursive self-improvement, which extends to artificial problem solvers that learn by learning.
In the 1990s, speaker Jürgen Schmidhuber created a variant of unsupervised adversarial neural networks. This played a minimax game to accomplish artificial curiosity, curiosity optimization, and curiosity augmentation. He also introduced the concept of Low-Complexity Art by generalizing algorithmic information theory and the many-worlds theory of physics.
